metadata.seed: He just can't. Anything that I say, he just disagrees. You know, he's right, I'm wrong. We can't come to common ground on anything. Curfew. He's just, you know, I feel I give him everything and he feels that we're too strict, you know? You know, and he wants to do things that I don't feel that is appropriate. But who knows? Just, who knows? What is he, what is he really wanting? But we've got. We've got to come to some type of common ground to figure out what we can do. Just make our life just a little bit easier and not fight all the time.

patient.query: He just can't. Anything that I say, he just disagrees. You know, he's right, I'm wrong. We can't come to common ground on anything. Curfew. He's just, you know, I feel I give him everything and he feels that we're too strict, you know? You know, and he wants to do things that I don't feel that is appropriate. But who knows? Just make our life just a little bit easier and not fight all the time.

automatic_thoughts: he wants to do inappropriate things

neutral: Tends to be intolerant of frustration (finding)

llm_response.response: "It sounds like you're having a hard time finding common ground with your son. Can you help me understand what specific things you both disagree on and why? This might help us identify some potential solutions."

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just… everything feels so tense between us lately. Like, I want to connect with him, but we just don’t see eye to eye on so many things. He seems to want freedom and independence, but I'm worried about him making the wrong choices. It just makes me feel like I’m failing as a parent.

automatic_thoughts: I want to connect with him

entailment: Worried about being a bad father (finding)

llm_response.response: I understand that things have been tense between you and your son, and it's natural to feel worried about his choices. It sounds like you deeply care for him and want the best for him. Let's explore these feelings together and see if we can find a way to strengthen your bond.

patient.query: I do care, so much. But sometimes, I feel like I’m just being overbearing, and then I get scared that he’ll push me away. I want him to know I’m here for him, but I also don’t want him to feel trapped. It’s confusing. I just wish we could… I don’t know, talk without arguing.

patient.query: Yeah, I suppose I do feel like I’m carrying a lot. There have been times, like when I tried to talk to him about his friends. I just wanted him to think things through, but it felt like I was suffocating him. I could see the frustration on his face, but I just couldn’t help myself. It’s like this voice in my head says I need to protect him, even if it pushes him away.

triggers: talking to him about his friends
automatic_thoughts: I could see the frustration on his face

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: I guess there have been moments when I felt a bit lighter, like when we can just hang out and do something fun together, without talking about serious stuff. Those times are great because it feels more natural, and I can see him relax. But then, after a little while, that voice comes back, and I start worrying about him again. It’s like I can’t fully enjoy those moments without something nagging at me.

Like, I see him hanging out with certain friends or skipping school, and I start imagining all the worst-case scenarios. It feels like if I don’t say something, I’ll regret it later. But I know that’s not always fair to him. It's just hard to let go of that worry.

that’s not always fair to him

I just don’t want to feel powerless in this.
